_readme: Golden retrieval-eval set (memory-refactor Stage 0; track closed 2026-07-09, residuals in HANDOFF 14.F). Each item: a question a user
might ask the reply bot, plus what search() should surface. Run: python tools/eval_retrieval.py
Match semantics: a returned chunk satisfies an item if it matches ANY entry in `expect`.
Within one expect entry, every specified field must match: `source_type` (exact),
at least one substring present in the chunk text).
Optional per-item `date_filter` / `entity_filter` are passed straight to search() — keep at
least one of each to exercise the filtered paths (Stage-1 date-filter fix; Stage-3a entity tags).
GROW THIS FILE as archived days accrue: add a few questions per interesting day, and add a
true 1-2 character ticker query (Stage-2 tokenizer case) when one first appears in the archive
— the 2026-06-30 day has no 1-2 char tickers, so ALM/AGI/WF6 are the closest exact-token probes.
Note: the WILTW report is indexed TWICE — the Opus summary (source_type 'wiltw') and the raw
PDF (source_type 'pdf', source_name 'Wiltw ...'). Both are correct answers for 13D questions,
Note on 'digest' chunks: the digest summarizes every source, so its chunks are topical matches
for almost any question — but the reply bot loads the day's digest separately as context, so
retrieval surfacing same-day digest chunks adds little. Default to expecting the UNDERLYING
source; only list a digest alternative where a digest-level answer is genuinely sufficient.
(This asymmetry is why the Stage-1 reranker scored nominally lower — it promotes digest chunks.)
